---
title: Customizing theme
description: Modify colors, typography, and other design tokens to match your brand.
---

Your theme defines design tokens for your project—reusable values for colors, typography, borders, corners, and shadows. Update a token once and every component using it updates automatically. Subframe generates a Tailwind CSS configuration with all your tokens.

## Viewing your theme

Navigate to theme settings:

1. Click **Theme** in the top navigation, or press <kbd>Cmd</kbd> + <kbd>K</kbd> and search "Theme"
2. Browse five sections: Colors, Typography, Borders, Corners, Shadows

All changes save automatically and apply immediately across your project.

## Editing your theme

### Theme presets

Apply professionally designed themes instantly:

1. Click **Edit theme** on the Theme page
2. Browse 10 preset styles: OpenAI, Linear, Stripe, Google, Airbnb, Vercel, Medium, Coinbase, Spotify, VS Code
3. Customize brand colors, fonts, and corner radius
4. Toggle between Light and Dark variants

Each preset configures color palettes (brand, neutral, error, success, warning), typography, and corner radius. Customize further after applying.

### Ask AI to generate theme

Use Ask AI to generate custom themes from a prompt:

1. Navigate to any page and open **Ask AI**
2. Describe your desired theme: "Create a retro theme with muted colors and bold typography"
3. Review the preview and click **Apply** to update your theme

<Note>AI theme generation affects your entire project. Use for major theme changes, not individual token adjustments.</Note>

## Colors

Subframe organizes colors into **palettes** and **individual tokens**.

### Palettes vs individual tokens

**Color palettes** group related shades with numbered suffixes (Brand 50, Brand 100... Brand 900):
- Based on Tailwind color presets
- Cannot reference other tokens (no aliases)
- Default palettes: Brand, Neutral, Error, Success, Warning
- Default palettes can be renamed but not deleted
- Custom palettes can be created and deleted

**Individual color tokens** are standalone semantic colors:
- Used for specific UI purposes: Brand Primary, Default Background, Neutral Border
- Can reference palette colors as aliases (e.g., Brand Primary → Brand 700)
- Custom tokens can be created and deleted
- Default tokens can be renamed but not deleted

Use palettes for color scales. Use individual tokens for semantic colors that reference palette shades.

```tsx
<div className="bg-neutral-100 text-default-font">
  Neutral background with default text color
</div>
```

### Editing colors

**Edit palette colors:** Click any palette color to open color picker.

**Swap entire palette:** Click palette icon next to palette name. Select from Tailwind color presets—all shades update instantly.

**Edit individual tokens:** Click any token to open color picker. Set direct color value or reference another token as alias.

### Creating colors

**New palette:**
1. Click **New palette** in Colors section
2. Name your palette
3. Add colors manually or import CSS variables (see [Importing tokens](/theme/importing-tokens))

**New individual token:**
1. Click **+** in individual tokens section
2. Name your token descriptively: `accent-purple`, `surface-elevated`
3. Set color via picker or reference existing token

**New palette color:**
1. Click **+** on palette row
2. Token auto-names with next suffix (100, 200, 300...)
3. Set color value

<Warning>Deleting a token converts all references to hex values. Components won't update when you change colors afterward.</Warning>

## Typography

Text tokens define typography styles with font family, size, weight, line height, and letter spacing.

```tsx
<span className="text-heading-1 font-heading-1">
  Heading 1
</span>
```

### Default tokens

Subframe includes eight default text tokens: Heading 1, Heading 2, Heading 3, Body Bold, Body, Caption Bold, Caption, Monospace Body. Default tokens can be renamed and modified but not deleted.

### Editing text tokens

Click any text token to edit:
- **Font size** — Pixel value
- **Line height** — Pixel value
- **Font weight** — Slider based on font's supported weights
- **Letter spacing** — Slider: -0.05em to 0.05em

### Changing font families

Click the font name card to select a different font family. This updates all tokens using that font. Choose from Google Fonts or custom uploaded fonts.

### Adding custom fonts

Upload WOFF, WOFF2, TTF, or OTF font files. Supports static fonts (one file per weight) and variable fonts (single file with weight range).

For detailed upload steps and codebase installation, see [Adding custom fonts](/theme/adding-custom-fonts).

### Creating text tokens

1. Click **+** in Typography section
2. Token copies properties from first existing token
3. Rename and adjust properties

Typography tokens auto-sort based on font size on the theme page.

## Borders

Border tokens define reusable border styles with color, width, and style.

```tsx
<div className="border-primary-border">
  Element with primary border style
</div>
```

### Editing border tokens

Click any border token to edit:
- **Border style** — Solid or dashed
- **Border size** — Pixel width
- **Border color** — Color token reference or direct value

### Creating border tokens

1. Click **+** in Borders section
2. Token copies default border properties
3. Rename and adjust style, size, color

## Corners

Corner tokens define border radius values for consistent rounded corners.

```tsx
<div className="rounded-medium">
  Element with medium corner radius
</div>
```

### Editing corner tokens

Click any corner token to edit radius value in pixels.

### Creating corner tokens

1. Click **+** in Corners section
2. Set radius value

Corner tokens auto-sort on the theme page.

## Shadows

Shadow tokens define box shadows with multiple layers for depth and elevation.

```tsx
<div className="shadow-small">
  Element with small shadow
</div>
```

### Editing shadow tokens

Click any shadow token to edit layers. Each layer has:
- **X/Y offset** — Horizontal and vertical offset in pixels
- **Blur radius** — Blur amount in pixels
- **Spread radius** — Spread amount in pixels
- **Color** — Shadow color with opacity
- **Inset** — Whether shadow appears inside element

Add multiple layers for complex shadow effects.

### Creating shadow tokens

1. Click **+** in Shadows section
2. Token copies default shadow properties
3. Add, remove, or edit layers

Shadow tokens auto-sort on the theme page.

## Syncing to code

After updating your theme, sync changes to your codebase:

```bash
npx @subframe/cli sync --all
```

This updates `tailwind.config.js` with new token values. Components automatically use the updated theme.

View generated Tailwind config by clicking **tailwind.config.js** at top of Theme page. Supports Tailwind v3 and v4 formats.

## Limitations

**Spacing not configurable** — Spacing tokens (padding, gaps) use Tailwind defaults. Extend `tailwind.config.js` manually for custom spacing.
